children.text: the amount and type of each such loan made;
the names and social security numbers of the borrowers;
the guaranty agency responsible for the guarantee of the loan;
the institution of higher education or organization responsible for loans made under parts D and E;

the exact amount of loans partially or totally canceled or in deferment for service under the Peace Corps Act ( et seq.), for service under the Domestic Volunteer Service Act of 1973 [ et seq.], and for comparable full-time service as a volunteer for a tax-exempt organization of demonstrated effectiveness;22 U.S.C. 250142 U.S.C. 4950
the eligible institution in which the student was enrolled or accepted for enrollment at the time the loan was made, and any additional institutions attended by the borrower;
the total amount of loans made to any borrower and the remaining balance of the loans;
the lender, holder, and servicer of such loans;

information concerning the date of any default on the loan and the collection of the loan, including any information concerning the repayment status of any defaulted loan on which the Secretary has made a payment pursuant to or the guaranty agency has made a payment to the previous holder of the loan;section 1080(a) of this title
information regarding any deferments or forbearance granted on such loans; and
the date of cancellation of the note upon completion of repayment by the borrower of the loan or payment by the Secretary pursuant to .section 1087 of this title
information concerning the income level of the borrower and his family and the extent of the borrower’s need for student financial assistance, including loans;
information concerning the type of institution attended by the borrower and the year of the program of education for which the loan was obtained;
information concerning other student financial assistance received by the borrower; and
information concerning Federal costs associated with the student loan program under part B, including the costs of interest subsidies, special allowance payments, and other subsidies.

children.heading: Verification
children.label: c Verification
children.text: The Secretary may require lenders, guaranty agencies, or institutions of higher education to verify information or obtain eligibility or other information through the National Student Loan Data System prior to making, guaranteeing, or certifying a loan made under part B, D, or E.

children.heading: Principles for administering the data system
children.label: d Principles for administering the data system

children.text: ensuring that the primary purpose of access to the data system by guaranty agencies, eligible lenders, and eligible institutions of higher education is for legitimate program operations, such as the need to verify the eligibility of a student, potential student, or parent for loans under part B, D, or E;
prohibiting nongovernmental researchers and policy analysts from accessing personally identifiable information;

children.label: A
children.text: informs the students that any subchapter IV grant or loan the students receive will be included in the National Student Loan Data System, and instructs the students on how to access that information;

children.label: B
children.text: describes the categories of individuals or entities that may access the data relating to such grant or loan through the data system, and for what purposes access is allowed;

children.label: C
children.text: defines and explains the categories of information included in the data system;

children.label: D
children.text: provides a summary of the provisions of (commonly known as the “Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974”) and other applicable Federal privacy statutes, and a statement of the students’ rights and responsibilities with respect to such statutes;section 1232g of this title

children.label: E
children.text: explains the measures taken by the Department to safeguard the students’ data; and

children.label: F
children.text: includes other information as determined appropriate by the Secretary;

children.label: A
children.text: submitted to the data system; and

children.label: B
children.text: accessible to guaranty agencies, eligible lenders, and eligible institutions of higher education determined by the Secretary to be authorized users of the data system;

children.label: A
children.text: delete inactive users from the data system;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the data in the data system are not being used for marketing purposes; and

children.label: C
children.text: monitor the use of the data system by guaranty agencies and eligible lenders to determine whether an agency or lender is accessing the records of students in which the agency or lender has no existing financial interest;

children.label: A
children.text: collecting data on the usage of the data system to monitor whether access has been or is being used contrary to the purposes of the data system;

children.label: B
children.text: defining the steps necessary for determining whether, and how, to deny or restrict access to the data system; and

children.label: C
children.text: determining the steps necessary to reopen access to the data system following a denial or restriction of access; and

preventing access to the data system and any other system used to administer a program under this subchapter by any person or entity for the purpose of assisting a student in managing loan repayment or applying for any repayment plan, consolidation loan, or other benefit authorized by this subchapter, unless such access meets the requirements described in subsection (e).

children.heading: Requirements for third-party data system access
children.label: e Requirements for third-party data system access
A
children.text: is in compliance with terms of service, information security standards, and a code of conduct which shall be established by the Secretary and published in the Federal Register;

children.label: B
children.text: is obtained using an access device (as defined in ) issued by the Secretary to the authorized person or entity; andsection 1029(e)(1) of title 18

children.label: C
children.text: is obtained without using any access device (as defined in ) issued by the Secretary to a student, borrower, or parent.section 1029(e)(1) of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: a guaranty agency, eligible lender, or eligible institution, or a third-party organization acting on behalf of a guaranty agency, eligible lender, or eligible institution, that is in compliance with applicable Federal law (including regulations and guidance); or

children.label: i
children.text: that attorney or other individual has never engaged in unfair, deceptive, or abusive practices, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: that attorney or other individual does not work for an entity that has engaged in unfair, deceptive, or abusive practices (including an entity that is owned or operated by a person or entity that engaged in such practices), as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: system access is provided only through a separate point of entry; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the attorney or other individual has consent from the relevant student, borrower, or parent to access the system.

children.label: A
children.text: the effectiveness of existing privacy safeguards in protecting student, borrower, and parent information in the data system;

children.label: B
children.text: the success of any new authorization protocols in more effectively preventing abuse of the data system;

children.label: C
children.text: the reduction in improper data system access as described in subsection (d)(7);

children.label: D
children.text: the ability of the Secretary to monitor how the system is being used, relative to the intended purposes of the data system; and

children.label: E
children.text: any protocols, codes of conduct, terms of service, or information security standards developed under paragraphs (6) or (7) of subsection (d) during the preceding fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: available mechanisms for providing students and parents with the ability to opt in or opt out of allowing eligible lenders to access their records in the National Student Loan Data System; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate protocols for limiting access to the data system, based on the risk assessment required under subchapter III of chapter 35 of title 44.

children.heading: Submission of study
children.label: B Submission of study
children.text: Not later than three years after , the Secretary shall prepare and submit a report on the findings of the study under subparagraph (A) to the authorizing committees.August 14, 2008

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulation prescribe standards and procedures (including relevant definitions) that require all lenders and guaranty agencies to report information on all aspects of loans made under this subchapter in uniform formats in order to permit the direct comparison of data submitted by individual lenders, servicers or guaranty agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with guaranty agencies, lenders, institutions of higher education, and organizations representing the groups described in paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: develop standards designed to be implemented by all guaranty agencies and lenders with minimum modifications to existing data processing hardware and software; and

children.label: C
children.text: publish the specifications selected to be used to encourage the automation of exchanges of information between all parties involved in loans under this subchapter.

revise the codes used to identify institutions and students in the student loan data system authorized by this section to make such codes consistent with the codes used in each database used by the Department of Education that contains information of participation in programs under this subchapter; and

modify the design or operation of the system authorized by this section to ensure that data relating to any institution is readily accessible and can be used in a form compatible with the integrated postsecondary education data system (IPEDS).

children.heading: Integration of databases
children.label: i Integration of databases
children.text: The Secretary shall integrate the National Student Loan Data System with the Pell Grant applicant and recipient databases as of , and any other databases containing information on participation in programs under this subchapter.January 1, 1994
